title: Checkpoint resume does not restore in-memory state — EVOLVE and IDENTITY degrade after crash recovery
description:
When the pipeline crashes and resumes via Checkpoint.find_resumable_run(), the runner calls _execute_from(resume_phase) which skips earlier phases. But the instance variables populated by those phases (self.trends, self.agent_results, self.newsletter_text, self.newsletter_eval, self.social_result, self.evolve_result) remain at their default empty values. The checkpoint.save() stores phase results as state_data in traces.db, but checkpoint.load() is never called during resume to restore them.

Impact:
- EVOLVE (runner.py:948-957) builds findings_per_agent from self.agent_results — empty after resume, so the analyzer prompt has no per-agent metrics and cannot identify underperforming agents.
- IDENTITY (runner.py:1059-1085) builds pipeline_results from self.social_result, self.newsletter_eval, self.agent_results, self.evolve_result — all empty after resume, so identity evolution has zero context about today's run.
- LEARN is safe (reads findings from DB directly at line 758).
- SYNTHESIS is safe (reads findings from DB directly at line 483).

This silently degrades pipeline self-improvement after every crash recovery.
requirements: In ResearchPipeline.run(), after finding a resumable run, load checkpoint state_data for each completed phase and restore the corresponding instance variables

Add a _restore_state_from_checkpoints(run_id) method that loads checkpoint data for TREND_SCAN (self.trends), RESEARCH (self.agent_results count), SYNTHESIS (self.newsletter_text path), LEARN (self.newsletter_eval), SOCIAL (self.social_result), EVOLVE (self.evolve_result)
For self.agent_results which stores complex AgentResult objects, store and restore a simplified dict with agent_name and findings_count so EVOLVE can use it
Log a warning if checkpoint state_data is missing for a completed phase (indicates old-format checkpoint)
done_criteria: After simulated crash at EVOLVE phase, resumed pipeline passes non-empty findings_per_agent to the analyzer prompt
After simulated crash at IDENTITY phase, resumed pipeline passes non-empty social_result to identity evolution
All existing tests pass (python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q)
context: The checkpoint system was added for crash recovery but only saves which phase completed — it never restores the data those phases produced. This means every crash recovery produces a degraded EVOLVE/IDENTITY pass. The fix should restore from checkpoint state_data, falling back gracefully if state_data is None (old checkpoints).
